result:
Done — all 4 tests pass unmodified, and the package no longer subscripts the config anywhere.

What changed:

- **`reportgen/config.py`** — Replaced the `DEFAULTS` dict with a frozen dataclass `Config` carrying the same four typed fields and defaults (`title: str = "Report"`, `output_format: str = "text"`, `max_rows: int = 10`, `include_summary: bool = True`). `load_config` now validates the JSON keys against the dataclass fields and raises `ValueError: Unknown config key: 'colour'` (naming the offending key) before constructing `Config(**raw)`, so file values still override defaults.
- **`reportgen/formatter.py`** — All `config["..."]` lookups switched to attribute access (`config.output_format`, `config.title`, `config.include_summary`).
- **`reportgen/loader.py`** — `config["max_rows"]` → `config.max_rows`.
- **`reportgen/main.py`** and `__init__.py` needed no changes — they only pass the config through.

Verified with `python3 -m pytest -q` (4 passed), a grep confirming no `cfg[`/`config[` remains under `reportgen/`, and a quick script exercising the unknown-key `ValueError`, default filling, and frozen immutability (`FrozenInstanceError` on assignment). Tests and git history untouched; no commits made.
